pages.raw_text:
The sixth annual convention of
the Spokane county w. c. T. U.
meets tomorrow and Friday at
Union Park M. e. church. A meet
ing of the executive committee is
called for a. m, which will be
followed by devotional service led
by Mrs. White, after which the
convention proper will be called to
order. At II o'clock the president,
Mrs. Eta N. Wooster, Spokane,
will deliver her annual address.
The afternoon session opens at
1870 and in the evening at 8
o'clock. This days program covers
reports and discussions of interest
to the organization in it's fight
against the liquor traffic. An ad-
dress of welcome by Rev. Lockhart,
short addresses on prohibition, and
music make up the evening pro-
gram. Officers will be elected Fri
day morning and memorial serv-
ices will be led by Mrs. Crippen.
Remaining reports and unfinished
business will be heard in this ses-
sion, with addresses and discus.
sions.


An excellent program of recita.
itions has been arranged for the
Demorest gold medal contest be-
ginning Friday night at 8. Con-
testants are Leland Argall, Ethel
Hindman, Beatrice Bradley, Jennie
Mortensen, Violet Hindman, Lillian
Estby.


The county organization enrolls
200 active workers, every point OF
Importance being represented by
white ribboners.
